content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 907 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration                    Executive/Legal           Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
1916 327 5600                                          322 6901                 327-4441

March 2, 1977

77-03-221

Mr. Eugene Portugal
2145 Everding Street
Eureka, California 95501

Dear Mr. Portugal:

This letter is in response to your letter of February
3, 1977, requesting an opinion as to whether or not you can
serve as a consultant to the local "Library Construction
Advisory Committee" and will confirm the advice given to
you by telephone on February 28, 1977.

The Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended, does not
bar public service in any particular capacity. It requires,
under specified circumstances, that a public official or
designated employee subject to a Conflict of Interest Code,
disclose the existence of specified types of financial
interests when it is reasonably foreseeable that there
interests will be materially financially affected by a
decision made or participated in by the official or desig-
nated employee. Accordingly, you are not barred by the Act
from serving as a consultant to a local "Library Construction Advisory Committee".

Further, as an employee of the city and/or county,
you may be a "public official" within the meaning of
Government Code §82048. Accordingly, you may be subject
to the prohibition applicable to all other public offi-
cials, contained in Government Code §97100, which reads
as follows:

No public official at any level of state or
local government shall make, participate in
making or in any way attempt to use his offi-
cial position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to
know he has a financial interest.

57100
BH-10

Eugene Portugal
March 2, 1977
Page Two


During the course of our telephone discussion, you
confirmed that the Library Construction Advisory Committee
has no authority to make decisions of any kind. Its func-
tions are solely advisory. You further confirmed that any
decision to create, fund and fill a consultant position
will be made by the city and county. The Committee's role
vis a vis the consultant is limited to giving advice only,
and you positioned yourself from the Committee when this issue
was discussed. Further, you confirm that you have no interests --
sources of income, interests in real property,
investment, or management position -- with the definition
of "financial interest" contained in Government Code
§87103, which would require you to disqualify yourself
from making or participating in making any governmental
decisions in your capacity as a consultant; should you be
hired for that position.

On the basis of these facts, it seems clear that you
are not barred from employment as a consultant to the
Library Construction Advisory Committee and that it is,
unlikely, because of your lack of "financial interests",
that you will be required to disqualify yourself from
making or participating in making any governmental
decisions in your capacity as a consultant to the
Advisory Committee.

I hope these informal remarks will be of some
assistance to you in making a decision in accepting
employment. Please let me know if I can be of further
assistance to you.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Edgar A. Kerly
Staff Attorney
Conflicts of Interest
Division

EAK:glb

embedding.qa_text: March 2, 1977 Mr. Eugene Portugal 2145 Everding Street Eureka, California 95501 Dear Mr. Portugal: This letter is in response to your letter of February 3, 1977, requesting an opinion as to whether or not you can serve as a consultant to the local "Library Construction Advisory Committee" and will confirm the advice given to you by telephone on February 28, 1977. The Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended, does not bar public service in any particular capacity. It requires, under specified circumstances, that a public official or designated employee subject to a Conflict of Interest Code, disclose the existence of specified types of financial interests when it is reasonably foreseeable that these interests will be materially financially affected by a decision made or participated in by the official or designated employee. Accordingly, you are not barred by the Act from serving as a consultant to a local "Library Construction Advisory Committee". Further, as an employee of the city and/or county, you may be a "public official" within the meaning of Government Code §82048. Accordingly, you may be subject to the prohibition applicable to all other public officials, contained in Government Code §87100, which reads as follows: No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. Eugene Portugal March 2, 1977 Page Two During the course of our telephone discussion, you confirmed that the Library Construction Advisory Committee has no authority to make decisions of any kind. Its functions are solely advisory. You further confirmed that any decision to create, fund and fill a consultant position will be made by the city and county. The Committee's role vis-a-vis the consultant is limited to giving advice only, and you absented yourself from the Committee when this issue was discussed. Further, you confirm that you have no interests -- sources of income, interests in real property, investments, or management positions -- within the definition of "financial interest" contained in Government Code §87103, which would require you to disqualify yourself from making or participating in making any governmental decisions in your capacity as a consultant, should you be hired for that position. On the basis of these facts, it seems clear that you are not barred from employment as a consultant to the Library Construction Advisory Committee and that it is unlikely, because of your lack of "financial interests", that you will be required to disqualify yourself from making or participating in making any governmental decisions in your capacity as a consultant to the Advisory Committee. I hope these informal remarks will be of some assistance to you in making a decision in accepting employment. Please let me know if I can be of further assistance to you. Sincerely, Edgar A. Kerry Staff Attorney Conflicts of Interest Division
